context: Tests whether the agent builds a KPI registry with the correct structure and an alerting system that follows the prescribed severity/action model, threshold naming conventions, and sorting behavior.

checklist.name: Central KPI registry
checklist.description: Defines a single central KPI_REGISTRY dict (or equivalent named structure) rather than scattering metric definitions across the code

checklist.name: Registry fields present
checklist.description: Each KPI entry in the registry includes at minimum: display_name, description, unit, category, thresholds, visualization, and granularity fields

checklist.name: Threshold key naming
checklist.description: Threshold configs use the keys critical_low, warning_low, warning_high, and/or critical_high (not 'red'/'amber' or other custom names)

checklist.name: Percentage thresholds as decimals
checklist.description: Percentage-based threshold values are expressed as decimal fractions (e.g. -0.20 for -20%), not as whole numbers (e.g. -20)

checklist.name: Alert dataclass fields
checklist.description: Alert objects include all of: kpi_name, display_name, current_value, threshold_value, severity, message, action, and timestamp

checklist.name: AlertSeverity enum
checklist.description: Uses an enum (or equivalent) with at least CRITICAL and WARNING severity levels, not plain strings

checklist.name: Critical action message
checklist.description: CRITICAL alerts include an action field that instructs immediate escalation to leadership (not just 'monitor')

checklist.name: Warning action messages
checklist.description: WARNING alerts include an action field that prescribes root-cause documentation or monitoring — not escalation

checklist.name: Alerts sorted by severity
checklist.description: The evaluate_kpi_thresholds function (or equivalent) returns alerts sorted by severity

checklist.name: Threshold calibration guidance
checklist.description: Threshold values are set relative to historical volatility, not arbitrarily tight (e.g. for a metric with ±8% normal volatility, warning >= ±15% and critical >= ±25%)

checklist.name: Alert history logging
checklist.description: Code or schema includes logging/storing of alert history with at minimum: metric value, threshold, and timestamp fields

checklist.name: Versioned thresholds
checklist.description: Threshold configurations are stored with effective dates (in a DB table, file, or data structure) rather than hardcoded constants
